audio_and_caption_sources.caption_text_policy: Visible rail captions and YouTube sidecars must be generated from the locked script text. ASR, SRT, VTT, and WhisperX artifacts may supply timing only.

story_time_model.music_contract_retime_note: A later music integration contract may add an intro voice-start offset; proof times and caption sidecars must be retimed there before rough assembly.

rail_text_model.active_panel: major chapter label, semantic active title, one short summary
rail_text_model.context_rows: nearby previous/upcoming semantic chapter titles only

rail_palette_intent.source_aware_palette: cold blue-gray Atlantic deck shadow, warm amber practical lamps, wet-deck reflections, muted cream caption text
rail_palette_intent.readability_strategy: localized active-panel fill and caption softener only; no broad full-frame dark veil and no full-height opaque right-column panel

chapters.title: Legal Answer, Wrong Question
chapters.rail_summary: The opening turns lawful lifeboat compliance into a warning.
chapters.story_anchor: Opening legal-compliance contradiction, familiar Titanic legend, and the thesis that the law was measuring the wrong thing.
chapters.visual_behavior: Start on the kept boat-deck plate with a settled rail state; covered lifeboats and damp deck reflections hold the legal-compliance premise.

chapters.title: The Highest Bracket Failed To Scale
chapters.rail_summary: The tonnage table gives a legal number that cannot cover the people aboard.
chapters.story_anchor: Merchant Shipping framework, highest tonnage bracket, sixteen boats, four collapsibles, and more than 2,200 people aboard.
chapters.visual_behavior: Rail emphasis shifts to repeated covered lifeboat/davit geometry; no tonnage table graphic or administrative prop appears.

chapters.title: Old Rules, New Ship
chapters.rail_summary: A category ceiling treats a giant liner like an older risk.
chapters.story_anchor: Old rules governing a new ship, category ceiling, and the bracket becoming too blunt to protect people.
chapters.visual_behavior: Subtle source crop favors ship scale, davit repetition, and empty deck capacity while the rail carries category language.

chapters.title: Compliance Became Sufficient
chapters.rail_summary: Administrative neatness turns a minimum into false adequacy.
chapters.story_anchor: Tonnage-table neatness, harder people-based questions, and the third layer where compliant becomes confused with sufficient.
chapters.visual_behavior: Right-rail typography carries the compliance/sufficiency turn; source art stays pre-disaster with no paperwork overlay.

chapters.title: The Davits Knew More
chapters.rail_summary: The ship's hardware makes a larger emergency physically thinkable.
chapters.story_anchor: Sixteen sets of double-acting davits, up to forty-eight boats, and the gap between what was thinkable and what was required.
chapters.visual_behavior: Attention favors covered lifeboats, falls, davit arms, rope tension, and wet deck light; no new boats or diagram overlays are painted in.

chapters.title: Capacity Became People
chapters.rail_summary: The evacuation separates too little capacity from failure to use even that capacity.
chapters.story_anchor: Boat accommodation used to less than two-thirds capacity, regulation failure versus evacuation failure, and category returning as bodies.
chapters.visual_behavior: Rail contrast separates capacity from evacuation without adding launch spectacle, panic crowds, bodies, wreckage, or sinking imagery.

chapters.title: The Rule Was Out Of Date
chapters.rail_summary: Parliament and the record move from one ship to the scale the rules had missed.
chapters.story_anchor: Post-sinking parliamentary debate, out-of-date regulations, old-risk regulation, and the gap acquiring a clock.
chapters.visual_behavior: Rail moves into aftermath language while the boat-deck carrier remains active; no hearing-room paperwork or source switch is introduced.

chapters.title: People, Not Tonnage
chapters.rail_summary: SOLAS and the closing receipts line turn the mechanism back to people aboard.
chapters.story_anchor: British Commissioner recommendation, SOLAS, the legend flattening the mechanism, and the receipts-lane sign-off.
chapters.visual_behavior: Motion and rail energy calm for synthesis; captions and story rail text suppress before the eventual end-screen target window.

typography_cues.trigger: Titanic had enough lifeboats to satisfy the law.
The law was measuring the wrong thing.
typography_cues.treatment: active summary shift toward measurement thesis
short rail emphasis paired with capacity context

typography_cues.trigger: the table had stopped tracking the scale
typography_cues.treatment: semantic title transition into category-ceiling frame
large rail phrase emphasis, no out-of-rail card
active summary resolves the compliance frame

typography_cues.trigger: That is the visual hinge of the episode.
typography_cues.treatment: rail title shift toward davits and physical readiness

typography_cues.trigger: what was thinkable and what was required
typography_cues.treatment: active context rows contrast possible versus required

typography_cues.trigger: One failure left too little capacity.
typography_cues.treatment: rail phrase emphasis before evacuation distinction

typography_cues.trigger: The ship turned that category back into bodies.
After the iceberg, the gap had a clock.
typography_cues.treatment: rail timing metaphor emphasis; no literal clock graphic

typography_cues.trigger: The law had stopped measuring the ships it was approving.

typography_cues.treatment: receipts-lane sign-off emphasis, then story text suppression

effect_moments.treatment: settled Candidate E plate, damp boat deck visible, covered lifeboats/davits held as visual premise, subtle rail entrance only

effect_moments.trigger: The law was measuring the wrong thing.
effect_moments.treatment: rail emphasis turns from famous disaster story to wrong measurement; no iceberg, sinking, map, or disaster spectacle added

effect_moments.trigger: The second layer is the category ceiling.
effect_moments.treatment: rail title transition and slight attention shift toward repeated lifeboat/davit geometry; no tonnage table graphic or out-of-rail card

effect_moments.trigger: The third layer is the most dangerous one.
effect_moments.treatment: active rail contrast tightens around compliance/sufficiency; localized readability treatment only, no paperwork/admin prop overlay

effect_moments.trigger: That is the visual hinge of the episode.
effect_moments.treatment: source-art attention favors covered lifeboats, falls, davit arms, and rope tension; no new boat geometry is painted in

effect_moments.trigger: That gap between what was thinkable and what was required
effect_moments.treatment: rail/context reordering and restrained source drift distinguish thinkable capacity from legal requirement; no connector lines or cyan traces

effect_moments.trigger: The fifth layer is the evacuation itself.
effect_moments.treatment: rail copy separates regulatory capacity from evacuation use; plate remains pre-disaster with no panic crowd or lifeboat launch spectacle

effect_moments.trigger: In Parliament, weeks after the sinking, the regulations were described as out of date.
effect_moments.treatment: rail moves into parliamentary aftermath without switching to hearing-room paperwork; public scene stays the boat-deck carrier

effect_moments.trigger: After Titanic, the institutional response moved toward a different frame.
effect_moments.treatment: rail title frames SOLAS/persons carried; caption text remains locked-script sourced, timing-only ASR sidecars stay non-authoritative
suppress captions and rail story text before future static YouTube end-screen target geometry

next_action: Human review must mark this cue map keep, tighten, or reject. A keep may open the living_cover_ambient_effects_layer gate.
